cases.title: DeJong v. Session of Village Seven Presbyterian Church

parties.respondent_or_court: Session of Village Seven Presbyterian Church (Rocky Mountain Presbytery)

cases.synopsis: RE DeJong complained against three actions of the Village Seven Presbyterian Church Session, including the erection of a Governance Commission and a Judicial Commission. The SJC denied the complaints regarding the bylaw change on elder terms and the judicial commission, but sustained the complaint against the governance commission, annulling Session's action, because the commission's broad scope and reversed delegation structure exceeded constitutional limits for an ecclesiastical commission.

cases.title: Acree v. Tennessee Valley Presbytery
precedent_refs: Case 2020-13, Benyola v. Central Florida (M48GA, 2021, p. 817)
Case 2020-01, Benyola v. Central Florida (M48GA, 2021, p. 801)
Case 2013-08, RE Warren Jackson v. Northwest Georgia (M43GA, 2015, p. 568)
Case 2012-06, Deacon Don Bethel v. Southeast Alabama (M41GA, 2013, p. 614)
Case 92-9b, Mr. Overman v. Eastern Carolina (M21GA, 1993, p. 223)
cases.synopsis: RE Acree attempted to file a BCO 43-1 complaint with Presbytery against an action taken at a Presbytery meeting he did not attend as a commissioner. The SJC found the case administratively out of order because Acree lacked standing, not having been a commissioner at the meeting where the action was taken.

cases.title: In the Matter of Korean Southwest Orange County Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC cited Korean Southwest Orange County Presbytery regarding unanswered RPR exception-of-substance citations from 2015-2018. The SJC extended a deadline for Presbytery to file adopted responses by April 1, 2022, threatening a called hearing if not met.

cases.title: Miller v. Ohio Valley Presbytery
cases.synopsis: Mr. Miller complained that the Session of Grace and Peace PCA erred in refusing to remove 14 items (stained glass windows, etchings, banners) he alleged violated the Second Commandment. The SJC denied the complaint, finding no clear error in the Session's judgment that the items were not images of the Triune God and were not used as aids in worship.

cases.title: Williams v. Chesapeake Presbytery
cases.synopsis: TE Williams appealed Chesapeake Presbytery's approval of his deposition from office and suspension from the Sacraments after trial on three charges (failure to manage household, slander, inappropriate behavior). The SJC denied all five specifications of error and upheld the Presbytery's decision in whole.

cases.title: Dudt v. Northwest Georgia Presbytery
precedent_refs: SJC 2015-11, Thompson v. S. Florida, M44GA, p. 539
SJC 1998-08 (Beverly Smith v. Southwest, M28GA, pp. 218, 227)
topics: express vs. implied will of session
cases.synopsis: RE Dudt was convicted and suspended from office by the Midway Presbyterian Church Session for emailing the congregation an SJC decision and expressing disagreement with a Session action, which the Session characterized as violating his ordination vows. The SJC sustained the appeal, reversing in whole and rendering a verdict of not guilty, finding that the Session had no constitutional authority to forbid Dudt from distributing the SJC decision and that the charges of false statements were not proven. A concurring opinion by several judges cautioned against elders emailing congregations in disagreement with Session decisions.
